meta.name: Blueberry Muffins (Grab-and-Go Bakery Item)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: Classic high-domed blueberry muffins with tender crumb and optional crunchy streusel. Includes both all-purpose flour and fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one). Designed as a grab-and-go breakfast bakery baseline with reliable mixing logic (do not overmix).

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (Option A — use this OR the fresh-milled option)
Fresh-milled flour, fine-milled (Option B — use this OR the AP option)
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled can absorb more; sift out the largest bran for a softer crumb. Batter may need 15–30 ml extra milk if thick.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly
Buttermilk (or plain yogurt thinned with milk)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/4 cups. If using yogurt, use 240 g yogurt + 60 ml milk.

ingredients.label: Extra milk (only if needed for fresh-milled flour batter)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is too thick; up to ~45 ml total depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Blueberries (fresh or frozen)
ingredients.notes: About 2 cups. If frozen, do not thaw.

ingredients.label: Flour (from your chosen flour) for tossing blueberries
Optional streusel topping (recommended for bakery style)
Flour (from your chosen flour) for streusel

steps.title: Prep oven and pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with liners or grease well. High initial heat helps create tall muffin tops.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the all-purpose flour OR the fresh-milled flour (not both). For fresh-milled flour, fine-mill and sift out the largest bran for best tenderness.

steps.title: Optional: make streusel topping
steps.body: If using streusel, combine streusel flour, brown sugar, cinnamon, and cold butter. Rub together until clumpy. Chill while making batter.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
steps.body: In a large bowl, whisk chosen flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and sugar.

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
steps.body: In a medium bowl, whisk eggs, melted butter, oil, buttermilk (or yogurt mixture), vanilla, and lemon zest if using.

steps.title: Combine (do not overmix)
steps.body: Pour wet into dry and fold with a spatula until just combined. Batter should be thick and lumpy; a few flour streaks are okay (they will hydrate during rest). Overmixing makes tough muffins.

steps.title: Adjust batter for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is overly thick (dry-looking), add extra milk 15 ml at a time until batter is thick but scoopable. Rest 5–10 minutes to hydrate, then fold once gently.

steps.title: Fold in blueberries
steps.body: Optional: toss blueberries with 1 tbsp flour (from your chosen flour) to help distribute. Gently fold blueberries into batter. If using frozen, fold quickly to limit color bleed.

steps.title: Portion batter
steps.body: Divide batter evenly among 12 cups (they should be quite full for tall tops). Top with streusel if using.

steps.title: Bake (hot start, then lower)
steps.body: Bake 6 minutes at 425°F (220°C), then reduce oven to 350°F (175°C) without opening the door. Bake 12–18 minutes more until tops are golden and a toothpick comes out clean or with a few moist crumbs.

steps.title: Cool
steps.body: Cool in pan 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack. Muffins are best slightly warm but set fully as they cool.

notes: Grab-and-go logic: Oil + butter keeps muffins tender across a few days; freeze well for quick breakfasts.
Dome logic: High initial oven heat creates rapid rise; lowering temp finishes baking without burning.
Fresh-milled flour: For best results, sift coarse bran and allow a short rest after mixing to hydrate. You may need a small milk adjustment.
Frozen blueberries: Do not thaw; fold gently and bake promptly to reduce bleeding.

storage.refrigerator: Airtight: up to 5 days (rewarm for best texture).
storage.freezer: Freeze airtight up to 2 months. Thaw at room temp or rewarm briefly.
